## Summary

Describe the change and whether it is normative, explanatory, schema-related, security-sensitive, implementation-specific, or governance-related.

## Problem addressed

Link the issue or RFC. Explain the failure without relying only on the proposed solution.

## Compatibility

- [ ] Additive and backward compatible
- [ ] Breaking change with migration plan
- [ ] Documentation only

Describe unknown-field and older-client behavior.

## Privacy impact

What data becomes public, encrypted, shared with a counterparty, shared with infrastructure, or retained locally?

## Security and abuse impact

Describe new threats, mitigations, residual risk, and failure behavior.

## Accessibility impact

Describe effects on accessible rides, assistive technology, language, cognitive load, or user warnings.

## Testing

- [ ] Schema validation
- [ ] Valid fixture
- [ ] Invalid fixture
- [ ] State transition tests
- [ ] Duplicate/reorder/replay tests
- [ ] Cross-client test or test plan
- [ ] Privacy lint review

## Evidence and prior art

Link primary sources and related implementations.

## Conflict of interest

Disclose relevant employment, funding, vendors, relay operations, payment interests, or token projects. Write `None` when not applicable.

## License and provenance

- [ ] I have the right to submit this work under Apache-2.0.
- [ ] Third-party material and required notices are identified.
- [ ] Employer, client, or corporate ownership does not prevent this contribution.
- [ ] Significant AI assistance that creates provenance uncertainty is disclosed.
- [ ] Every commit includes a Developer Certificate of Origin `Signed-off-by` line.

## Checklist

- [ ] No real private keys, identities, exact trips, or contact details are included.
- [ ] Claims of safety, privacy, decentralization, and reliability are appropriately qualified.
- [ ] The change does not introduce an undocumented mandatory service.
- [ ] Documentation, schemas, examples, and tests agree.
